parsed_review.summary: CP00-789 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk B, 40 units, RP26.P01.M02.S09-RP26.P01.M04.S06) passes independent read-only review. The pack-level production_ready claim is scoped to enterprise_saas_domain_model_workflow_descriptor_verified (descriptor layer), explicitly NOT runtime_ready and NOT an enterprise-trust claim, and that scoped claim is well supported. Implementation (packages/enterprise/src/{registry,service,validators,sso,scim,index}.js) is descriptor-only and extends CP00-788; ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION pins all runtime/leak flags false and validators enforce no tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads, no cross-tenant routing, no runtime opening, and no enterprise-trust claim. A flag sweep across the contract JSON and source found zero dispatches_/exposes_/writes_product_state/claims_enterprise_trust/runtime_* set to true. Local/Hermes validation is complete: command-evidence shows all 15 commands at exit_code 0, including npm test (3052 pass / 0 fail with CP00-789-specific coverage, descriptor, and contract tests), rp26:enterprise-saas:validate (pack CP00-789), and git diff --check clean. Authority boundary is correct: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), with human approval required for runtime opening; Hermes patterns are confined to descriptor-only evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Manifest, adjudication, construction-inspection, and claude-review-result are correctly in pre-review pending state with manifest production_ready=false (no premature claim), matching the validated CP00-788 close pattern; this review is the gate that enables adjudication. Review-evidence hardening checks pass (auth preflight exit 0 with 1226-byte valid JSON, schema present, forbidden tools absent, prompt under size limit, no prior invalid attempts). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. blocks_goal_closeout is false: the expected human/enterprise-trust authority boundary lies outside this descriptor receipt and the pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust.
